mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when UNRATE crosses the 9.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above the 100 index baseline signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative in >55% of observations when INDPRO > 100, or the mechanism reverses direction during late-cycle inventory accumulation phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 217.0 signal accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to predict direction with a Rank IC > 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations despite payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% quarter-over-quarter indicates stagnating aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when GDP growth crosses the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indices reflect robust manufacturing output and supply chain throughput, which directly supports short-term corporate revenue realization and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations, or the signal fails to achieve a Rank IC > 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
